clues.tournamentHint: Hosts the PGA Tour's annual celebrity pro-am and has hosted six U.S. Opens
clues.didYouKnow: The course was designed by two amateur golfers, Jack Neville and Douglas Grant, neither of whom had ever designed a golf course before—yet their creation consistently ranks among the greatest courses in the world.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the 2000 U.S. Open by a record 15 strokes, finishing at 12-under par
Tom Watson chipped in on the 17th hole to beat Jack Nicklaus in the 1982 U.S. Open
Jack Nicklaus hit the flagstick with a 1-iron on the par-3 17th hole in the 1972 U.S. Open
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 7: A dramatic 106-yard par 3 that plays sharply downhill to a tiny green perched on a rocky promontory jutting into Carmel Bay, often regarded as one of the most photographed holes in golf.
reveal.description: Pebble Beach Golf Links hugs the rugged coastline of the Monterey Peninsula, offering some of the most breathtaking ocean views in all of golf. With eight holes running along the Pacific, the course blends raw natural beauty with strategic championship-caliber design, making it a bucket-list destination for golfers worldwide.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of The Masters, golf's most prestigious invitational tournament
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built on the site of Fruitland Nurseries, a former commercial plant nursery, which is why the course is adorned with flowering plants and every hole is named after a tree or shrub.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the 1997 Masters by a record 12 strokes at age 21, his first major championship
Jack Nicklaus won his sixth Green Jacket in 1986 at age 46 with a legendary back-nine charge
Tiger Woods completed one of sport's greatest comebacks by winning the 2019 Masters, his first major in 11 years
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 12: 'Golden Bell,' a 155-yard par 3 at the heart of Amen Corner. The deceptively short hole plays over Rae's Creek, with swirling winds that make club selection agonizing and have produced some of the most dramatic moments in Masters history.
reveal.description: Augusta National is the cathedral of American golf, a meticulously manicured private club that hosts The Masters each April. Co-designed by Bobby Jones and Alister MacKenzie, the course features dramatic elevation changes, blazing-fast greens, and the iconic Amen Corner stretch that has defined championship golf for nearly a century.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of The Players Championship, often called golf's 'fifth major'
clues.didYouKnow: Approximately 120,000 golf balls are retrieved from the water surrounding the famous island green 17th hole each year.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods' iconic chip-in on the 17th hole during the 2001 Players Championship, where the ball paused on the lip before dropping in
Rickie Fowler birdied three of the last four holes to force a playoff and win the 2015 Players Championship
Fred Couples hit one of the most memorable shots in tournament history, holing out from the fairway on 18 to win in 1996
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: The most famous par 3 in golf, a 137-yard shot to a small island green surrounded entirely by water. It produces more drama per square foot than any hole in professional golf.
reveal.description: TPC Sawgrass is the home of the PGA Tour and the annual Players Championship. Pete Dye's Stadium Course pioneered the concept of spectator-friendly golf architecture, with mounding and amphitheater-style viewing areas throughout. The island green 17th hole is arguably the most recognizable single hole in the sport.

clues.tournamentHint: First course to host back-to-back U.S. Open and U.S. Women's Open in the same year (2014)
clues.didYouKnow: In 2014, This course became the first course in history to host both the U.S. Open and U.S. Women's Open in back-to-back weeks on the same course setup.

famousMoments: Payne Stewart sank a dramatic 15-foot par putt on the 18th hole to win the 1999 U.S. Open, captured in one of golf's most iconic fist-pump celebrations
Martin Kaymer dominated the 2014 U.S. Open, building an eight-shot lead through 36 holes
Michael Campbell upset Tiger Woods to win the 2005 U.S. Open
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 5: A 478-yard par 4 that Donald Ross reportedly considered his finest creation, featuring one of his signature crowned greens that repels anything less than a perfectly struck approach shot.
reveal.description: Pinehurst No. 2 is Donald Ross's masterpiece and the crown jewel of the Pinehurst Resort's nine courses. Set among the longleaf pines and sandy wiregrass of the North Carolina Sandhills, the course is defined by its famous crowned greens—convex putting surfaces that demand precision and creativity around the greens.

clues.tournamentHint: Has hosted more U.S. Opens than any other course in history (nine times)
clues.didYouKnow: The course's famous Church Pews bunker complex between the 3rd and 4th fairways contains a series of twelve grass-covered ridges inside a massive sand trap, creating one of golf's most unique and intimidating hazards.

famousMoments: Johnny Miller fired a final-round 63 to win the 1973 U.S. Open, considered one of the greatest rounds in major championship history
Dustin Johnson won the 2016 U.S. Open despite a controversial ruling on the 5th green regarding a ball that moved
Ben Hogan defeated Sam Snead in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1953 U.S. Open
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 3: A 428-yard par 4 bordered by the legendary Church Pews bunker complex on the left, a massive hazard featuring twelve parallel rows of grassy ridges that can trap and punish even the slightest miss.
reveal.description: Oakmont Country Club is widely regarded as the toughest course in America, a relentless test of precision featuring lightning-fast greens, deep furrowed bunkers, and almost no rough relief. Founded in 1903 by Henry Fownes, it has hosted more combined major championships than any other American venue.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Farmers Insurance Open and site of Tiger Woods' legendary 2008 U.S. Open victory
clues.didYouKnow: As a municipal course owned by the City of San Diego, any golfer can play The course for a green fee, making it arguably the most accessible major championship venue in golf.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the 2008 U.S. Open in a playoff against Rocco Mediate while playing on a broken left leg and torn ACL, hobbling through 91 holes over five days
Jon Rahm holed a birdie putt on the 72nd hole to win the 2021 U.S. Open by one stroke
Tiger Woods has won the Farmers Insurance Open (and predecessor events) a record eight times at Torrey Pines
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 3: A 200-yard par 3 perched atop the coastal bluffs with sweeping views of the Pacific Ocean 300 feet below, requiring a precise tee shot over a deep ravine to a green guarded by bunkers.
reveal.description: Torrey Pines South Course sits on dramatic coastal bluffs overlooking the Pacific Ocean in La Jolla, California. Redesigned by Rees Jones for the 2008 U.S. Open, the municipal course proves that public golf can produce world-class championship conditions, combining stunning ocean panoramas with a demanding, links-influenced layout.

clues.tournamentHint: First publicly owned course to host the U.S. Open in the modern era (2002)
clues.didYouKnow: A famous sign on the first tee reads: 'WARNING: The Black Course Is an Extremely Difficult Course Which We Recommend Only for Highly Skilled Golfers.' It is one of five courses in the state park system, all open to any New York resident.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods dominated the 2002 U.S. Open, winning by three strokes as massive New York crowds gave the event an electrifying atmosphere
Brooks Koepka held off Dustin Johnson to win the 2019 PGA Championship by two strokes
Lucas Glover won the rain-soaked 2009 U.S. Open, one of the wettest U.S. Opens in history
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 4: A 517-yard par 5 that is the course's most visually intimidating hole, with an elevated tee shot that must carry deep cross-bunkers, a narrow landing area flanked by dense rough, and a severely contoured green.
reveal.description: Bethpage Black is the people's championship course—a brutally difficult public layout on Long Island that proved a state park course could host golf's biggest events. A.W. Tillinghast's muscular design features deep bunkers, punishing rough, and relentless length, all set amid the rolling terrain of Bethpage State Park.

clues.tournamentHint: One of the five founding member clubs of the USGA, hosted one of the earliest U.S. Opens in 1896
clues.didYouKnow: This course is one of the five founding member clubs of the USGA (1894) and is the oldest incorporated golf club in the United States. Its original clubhouse, designed by Stanford White, was the first purpose-built golf clubhouse in America.

famousMoments: The 2004 U.S. Open became controversial when the USGA lost control of the 7th green on Saturday, which dried out and became nearly unplayable
Brooks Koepka successfully defended his U.S. Open title at Shinnecock Hills in 2018, winning by one stroke over Tommy Fleetwood
Raymond Floyd won the 1986 U.S. Open at Shinnecock at age 43, one of the oldest champions in tournament history
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 7: A 189-yard par 3 that epitomizes Shinnecock's windswept character. The elevated tee offers panoramic views of Peconic Bay and the Atlantic, while the shallow green, flanked by deep bunkers, demands a perfectly judged shot in the ever-present ocean breeze.
reveal.description: Shinnecock Hills is the quintessential American links course, set on the wind-swept dunes of Long Island's eastern end near the Atlantic Ocean. William Flynn's 1931 redesign created a masterpiece of strategic golf architecture that rewards creativity and punishes bravado, with its firm, fast conditions and ever-present ocean winds.

clues.tournamentHint: A frequent PGA Championship venue designed by the Golden Bear specifically for major championships
clues.didYouKnow: Jack Nicklaus designed the course with the express purpose of creating a major championship venue, and the reachable par-5 18th hole was intentionally built to produce dramatic, birdie-or-bust finishes in tournament play.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods defeated Bob May in a thrilling three-hole playoff to win the 2000 PGA Championship after May nearly holed out on 18 in regulation
Rory McIlroy held off Phil Mickelson and Rickie Fowler to win the 2014 PGA Championship in near-darkness on Sunday evening
The United States won the 2008 Ryder Cup at Valhalla, their first victory since 1999
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 542-yard par 5 that is reachable in two for long hitters, with Floyds Fork creek running along the right side and guarding the green. This hole has produced countless dramatic eagles and birdies in PGA Championship finishes.
reveal.description: Valhalla Golf Club in Louisville, Kentucky, was designed by Jack Nicklaus as a purpose-built championship venue and has delivered on that promise with four PGA Championships and a Ryder Cup. The course winds through mature hardwood forests along Floyds Fork creek, with generous fairways that reward aggressive play and a thrilling closing stretch.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the WM Phoenix Open, the best-attended event in all of golf
clues.didYouKnow: The 16th hole during the WM Phoenix Open features a temporary stadium holding over 20,000 spectators, creating the rowdiest atmosphere in professional golf—more akin to a football game than a golf tournament.

notableTournaments: WM Phoenix Open (annually, formerly the Phoenix Open)
famousMoments: Tiger Woods made a hole-in-one on the 16th hole during the 1997 Phoenix Open, sending the stadium crowd into a frenzy of thrown beer cups and deafening cheers
Sam Ryder aced the 16th hole during the 2022 WM Phoenix Open, unleashing another legendary crowd eruption
The WM Phoenix Open regularly draws over 700,000 spectators for the week, making it the best-attended sporting event in the world
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A 163-yard par 3 enclosed by a massive temporary stadium grandstand that transforms it into the loudest, most electric hole in golf during the WM Phoenix Open. Players are cheered, jeered, and serenaded like rock stars.
reveal.description: TPC Scottsdale's Stadium Course is the home of the WM Phoenix Open, golf's biggest party. Set in the Sonoran Desert with views of the McDowell Mountains, the course was designed to accommodate enormous galleries, but it's the raucous 16th hole stadium that has made this venue a cultural phenomenon in professional golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Site of the 'Massacre at the course' and six U.S. Opens on its famed West Course
clues.didYouKnow: The 1974 U.S. Open at This course was dubbed the 'Massacre at the course' when Hale Irwin won at 7-over par—at the time, the highest winning score relative to par in modern U.S. Open history.

famousMoments: Phil Mickelson's double bogey on the 72nd hole of the 2006 U.S. Open, when he needed only a par to win, remains one of golf's most heartbreaking collapses
Bryson DeChambeau overpowered the West Course to win the 2020 U.S. Open at 6-under, the only player under par
Hale Irwin's 'Massacre at Winged Foot' victory in 1974 at 7-over par
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 474-yard par 4 where Phil Mickelson's infamous 2006 U.S. Open collapse occurred. The uphill approach to a heavily bunkered, crowned green has decided multiple U.S. Opens and is one of the most pressure-packed finishing holes in championship golf.
reveal.description: Winged Foot's West Course is an A.W. Tillinghast masterwork in the suburbs of New York City, known for its demanding length, small crowned greens, and deep bunkering. The course has been the site of some of golf's most dramatic moments and is widely considered one of the ultimate tests in U.S. Open golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Where Bobby Jones completed the Grand Slam in 1930 and Ben Hogan hit his famous 1-iron in 1950
clues.didYouKnow: the course uses wicker baskets atop its flagsticks instead of traditional flags, a distinctive tradition dating back over a century. It is also one of the shortest courses to host a modern U.S. Open at under 7,000 yards.

famousMoments: Ben Hogan struck his legendary 1-iron approach to the 18th green in the 1950 U.S. Open, just 16 months after a near-fatal car accident, and won in a playoff
Bobby Jones completed the original Grand Slam at Merion by winning the 1930 U.S. Amateur
Justin Rose holed a clutch par putt on the final hole to win the 2013 U.S. Open, his first major title
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 11: A 370-yard par 4 known as the 'Wee Burn Hole,' featuring Cobbs Creek crossing in front of the green. This short but treacherous hole demands a perfectly positioned tee shot and precise approach over the creek to a shallow, well-guarded green.
reveal.description: Merion Golf Club's East Course in the Philadelphia suburbs is a compact masterpiece that proves a championship course doesn't need to be 7,500 yards. Hugh Wilson's ingenious design packs an extraordinary test into a relatively small property, with creative bunkering, its signature wicker basket flagsticks, and a rich history that includes some of golf's most iconic moments.

clues.tournamentHint: Washington D.C.'s premier championship venue, where Rory McIlroy set a U.S. Open scoring record in 2011
clues.didYouKnow: Every U.S. President from Calvin Coolidge to John F. Kennedy held honorary memberships at the course, making it the most politically connected golf club in American history.

famousMoments: Rory McIlroy obliterated the field at the 2011 U.S. Open, winning by eight strokes at 16-under par after setting the 72-hole U.S. Open scoring record
Ken Venturi won the 1964 U.S. Open in brutal heat, nearly collapsing from heat exhaustion during the 36-hole final day
Ernie Els won the 1997 U.S. Open at Congressional, his second U.S. Open title in four years
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A demanding 483-yard par 4 with water guarding the front and right of the green. The uphill approach shot over the pond has decided multiple championships and creates a dramatic amphitheater finish.
reveal.description: Congressional Country Club sits just outside Washington, D.C., in the affluent suburbs of Bethesda, Maryland. Originally designed by Devereux Emmet and later modified by Robert Trent Jones Sr. and Rees Jones, the Blue Course is a long, demanding test that has hosted three U.S. Opens and served as a social centerpiece for the nation's political elite.

clues.tournamentHint: Oklahoma's premier major championship venue, hosting multiple U.S. Opens and PGA Championships
clues.didYouKnow: Perry Maxwell's greens at This course feature his trademark 'Maxwell Rolls'—subtle, deceptive undulations in the putting surfaces that are widely considered among the finest set of greens in championship golf.

famousMoments: Justin Thomas won the 2022 PGA Championship in a playoff over Will Zalatoris after coming from seven shots back in the final round
Tiger Woods won the 2007 PGA Championship in sweltering Oklahoma heat, his 13th major title
Retief Goosen won the 2001 U.S. Open at Southern Hills after a final-round 70
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 12: A 458-yard par 4 that plays as one of the most demanding two-shotters in major championship golf, featuring a narrow, tree-lined fairway and one of Maxwell's most severe crowned greens with a false front that repels weak approach shots.
reveal.description: Southern Hills Country Club in Tulsa is Perry Maxwell's crowning achievement, a course celebrated for its subtly brilliant green complexes and relentless shotmaking demands. Despite Oklahoma's relatively flat terrain, Maxwell carved out a varied, challenging layout through creek-laced hardwood forests that has hosted more major championships than any other course in the region.

clues.tournamentHint: Historic New Jersey club that has hosted seven U.S. Opens and multiple PGA Championships on its Lower Course
clues.didYouKnow: The club is named after Baltus Roll, a Dutch farmer who was murdered on the property in 1831 by two thieves. The crime was so notorious that it gave the surrounding area its name.

famousMoments: Jack Nicklaus hit a famous 1-iron to the 18th green in the final round of the 1967 U.S. Open, clinching his second U.S. Open title
Phil Mickelson won the 2005 PGA Championship at Baltusrol with a dramatic final-round flop shot and birdie on the 72nd hole
Jimmy Walker held off Jason Day to win the 2016 PGA Championship for his first major title
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 4: A 194-yard par 3 on the Lower Course, featuring a large, severely contoured green guarded by deep bunkers. It has been a pivotal hole in multiple U.S. Opens and PGA Championships throughout the club's storied history.
reveal.description: Baltusrol Golf Club in Springfield, New Jersey, is one of America's most historic championship venues. A.W. Tillinghast redesigned the club's courses in 1922, creating the Lower Course that has become one of the most decorated major championship stages in golf. The course is known for its dramatic par 3s and demanding finishing stretch.

clues.tournamentHint: Known as the 'Graveyard of Champions' for its history of stunning U.S. Open upsets
clues.didYouKnow: This course is called the 'Graveyard of Champions' because tournament favorites consistently lose there—Ben Hogan, Arnold Palmer, Tom Watson, and Payne Stewart all suffered shocking defeats in U.S. Opens at the Lake Course.

famousMoments: Unknown municipal course pro Jack Fleck stunned Ben Hogan in an 18-hole playoff to win the 1955 U.S. Open, one of golf's greatest upsets
Billy Casper erased a seven-shot deficit over the final nine holes to tie Arnold Palmer, then won the 1966 U.S. Open playoff
Webb Simpson won the 2012 U.S. Open as the course claimed more star victims, with Tiger Woods and others fading on the weekend
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 347-yard par 4 that plays uphill through a chute of towering Monterey cypress trees. Despite its short yardage, the narrow fairway, overhanging trees, and slick green have decided multiple U.S. Opens.
reveal.description: The Olympic Club's Lake Course is nestled in the foggy, cypress-lined terrain of San Francisco's western edge near the Pacific Ocean. Despite lacking water on the Lake Course itself (the name comes from the adjacent Lake Merced), the tight, tree-lined layout has earned its reputation as a championship spoiler where favorites falter and underdogs triumph.

clues.tournamentHint: Site of Europe's 'Miracle at the course' 2012 Ryder Cup comeback and three U.S. Opens
clues.didYouKnow: The course's iconic clubhouse features a massive Byzantine-inspired dome and is one of the largest and most ornate clubhouse structures in all of golf, originally built by the course Shriners as a retreat.

famousMoments: Europe staged the greatest comeback in Ryder Cup history at the 2012 'Miracle at Medinah,' rallying from a 10-6 deficit entering Sunday singles to win 14.5 to 13.5
Tiger Woods won back-to-back PGA Championships at Medinah in 1999 and 2006, with the 2006 victory coming shortly after his father's death
Hale Irwin won the 1990 U.S. Open at Medinah at age 45, celebrating with a high-five sprint around the 18th green
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A 193-yard par 3 that plays over Lake Kadijah, with the clubhouse dome visible in the background. Martin Kaymer holed the winning putt for Europe on this green during the 2012 Ryder Cup's dramatic conclusion.
reveal.description: Medinah Country Club's Course No. 3 is a demanding, heavily wooded layout west of Chicago, known for its length, its signature lake holes, and one of the most stunning clubhouse backdrops in golf. The course has been the stage for some of the most memorable moments in Ryder Cup and major championship history.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2016 Ryder Cup, multiple PGA Championships, and U.S. Opens in Minnesota
clues.didYouKnow: When the U.S. Open first came to the course in 1970, Dave Hill famously quipped the course 'lacked only 80 acres of corn and a few cows' to be a farm—prompting significant design changes that transformed it into a world-class championship venue.

famousMoments: The United States demolished Europe 17-11 at the 2016 Ryder Cup, powered by an inspired performance from Patrick Reed, who earned the nickname 'Captain America'
Y.E. Yang stunned Tiger Woods at the 2009 PGA Championship, becoming the first player to come from behind to beat Tiger in a major's final round
Payne Stewart won the 1991 U.S. Open at Hazeltine, defeating Scott Simpson in an 18-hole playoff
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A 403-yard par 4 that doglegs left around Hazeltine Lake, with water threatening the entire left side from tee to green. It was a pivotal hole during the 2016 Ryder Cup and demands both courage and precision.
reveal.description: Hazeltine National Golf Club is Minnesota's premier championship venue, a Robert Trent Jones Sr. design built around the shores of Hazeltine Lake near Minneapolis. After early criticism, the course underwent significant modifications that transformed it into one of the most respected major championship venues in the country, hosting Ryder Cups, PGA Championships, and U.S. Opens.

details.architect: Michael Hurdzan, Dana Fry & Ron Whitten

clues.architectHint: Michael Hurdzan, Dana Fry & Ron Whitten
clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2017 U.S. Open, one of the newest courses to ever host the championship
clues.didYouKnow: This course features over 150 feet of elevation change across glacial drumlin terrain, with knee-high fescue rough that gives the Wisconsin course an appearance reminiscent of Irish links golf.

famousMoments: Brooks Koepka dominated the 2017 U.S. Open, finishing at 16-under par—the lowest score in relation to par in U.S. Open history at the time
Justin Thomas shot a record-tying 63 in the third round of the 2017 U.S. Open, the lowest round ever in a U.S. Open (later tied)
The 2017 U.S. Open was one of the most wide-open in history, with seven players finishing within four shots of the lead
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 669-yard par 5 that sweeps downhill through a valley of fescue-covered drumlins, offering long hitters a chance to reach the green in two but punishing anything offline with dense native grasses and a fiercely contoured green complex.
reveal.description: Erin Hills is a bold, modern design carved through the glacial terrain of southeastern Wisconsin. The course's dramatic elevation changes, sweeping fescue-covered drumlin hills, and wide fairways with penal rough create a distinctly links-like experience in America's heartland. It became one of the newest and longest courses to host a U.S. Open in 2017.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2015 U.S. Open, the first major championship held in the Pacific Northwest
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built on a former 930-acre sand and gravel mine alongside Puget Sound, making it one of the most dramatic land reclamation projects in golf history.

famousMoments: Jordan Spieth won the 2015 U.S. Open at age 21, his second consecutive major championship victory
Dustin Johnson three-putted the 18th green from 12 feet in the final round of the 2015 U.S. Open, missing a chance to force a playoff by one stroke
The 2015 U.S. Open was the first major championship ever held in the Pacific Northwest
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: A 172-yard par 3 with a dramatic view of Puget Sound and the distant Olympic Mountains. The lone tree standing sentinel beside the green has become the course's most iconic visual landmark.
reveal.description: Chambers Bay is a links-style municipal course built in a former sand and gravel quarry on the shores of Puget Sound in Washington state. Robert Trent Jones Jr.'s design features dramatic elevation changes, fescue fairways, and a single tree on the entire property, creating a rugged, windswept landscape that brought major championship golf to the Pacific Northwest for the first time.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted three PGA Championships and the record-setting 2021 Ryder Cup along the shores of Lake Michigan
clues.didYouKnow: Pete Dye imported over 13,000 truckloads of sand to transform a flat former military site along Lake Michigan into a course resembling the rugged links of Ireland, complete with more than 1,000 bunkers scattered across the property.

famousMoments: Dustin Johnson was penalized two strokes on the 72nd hole of the 2010 PGA Championship for grounding his club in what he didn't realize was a bunker, costing him a potential playoff
Team USA crushed Europe 19-9 at the 2021 Ryder Cup, the largest margin of victory in the competition's modern history
Jason Day won the 2015 PGA Championship at a then-record 20-under par, the lowest score in major championship history at the time
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A 223-yard par 3 perched on cliffs high above Lake Michigan, with a sheer drop to the beach on the right and bunkers left. The wind off the lake makes club selection a guessing game and the views are among the most spectacular in American golf.
reveal.description: Whistling Straits is Pete Dye's audacious Irish links fantasy along the shores of Lake Michigan in Wisconsin. Built on dramatically reshaped terrain, the course features over 1,000 bunkers, rugged dune-like mounding, and sweeping lake views that make it feel more like County Kerry than Sheboygan County. Part of the Kohler resort complex, it has quickly become one of America's most iconic championship venues.

clues.tournamentHint: Site of the dramatic 'War by the Shore' 1991 Ryder Cup and multiple PGA Championships
clues.didYouKnow: Pete Dye's wife, Alice, convinced him to raise the fairways and greens above the dunes so that the Atlantic This course would be visible from every hole—a last-minute design change that defined the course's character.

famousMoments: Bernhard Langer missed a six-foot putt on the 18th hole that would have retained the Ryder Cup for Europe in the 1991 'War by the Shore,' giving the United States a dramatic one-point victory
Phil Mickelson became the oldest major champion in history by winning the 2021 PGA Championship at age 50
Rory McIlroy won the 2012 PGA Championship by eight strokes in dominant fashion
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A 197-yard par 3 with the Atlantic Ocean as an immediate backdrop, featuring a green perched among the dunes and exposed to fierce coastal winds that can change a 7-iron into a 4-iron between rounds.
reveal.description: The Ocean Course at Kiawah Island is Pete Dye's windswept masterpiece on a barrier island off the South Carolina coast. Every hole offers views of the Atlantic Ocean or the marshlands, and the ever-present sea breezes transform an already challenging layout into one of the most demanding tests in championship golf. It burst onto the world stage hosting the dramatic 1991 Ryder Cup.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2018 PGA Championship and the 1965 U.S. Open in suburban St. Louis
clues.didYouKnow: This course relocated from its original St. Louis city location to its current suburban site in 1960, and it was the last Robert Trent Jones Sr. design to host a U.S. Open when Gary Player won the 1965 championship there.

famousMoments: Brooks Koepka won the 2018 PGA Championship by two strokes over Tiger Woods, who mounted a thrilling Sunday charge that electrified the massive St. Louis galleries
Gary Player won the 1965 U.S. Open at Bellerive in a playoff over Kel Nagle, completing his career Grand Slam
Tiger Woods' Sunday charge at the 2018 PGA drew some of the largest and most enthusiastic crowds in PGA Championship history
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 470-yard par 4 that finishes with an approach shot to a green guarded by water on the left. Brooks Koepka's closing birdie here in 2018 sealed his PGA Championship victory as Tiger Woods watched from the fairway behind.
reveal.description: Bellerive Country Club is a Robert Trent Jones Sr. design in the western suburbs of St. Louis, known for its length, lush conditioning, and tree-lined fairways. The course gained renewed prominence as the site of the 2018 PGA Championship, where Tiger Woods' electric Sunday charge captivated the sporting world and signaled his return to major championship contention.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Wells Fargo Championship and host of the 2017 PGA Championship in Charlotte
clues.didYouKnow: The final three holes at the course—known as the 'Green Mile'—are statistically among the most difficult closing stretches on the entire PGA Tour, named after the nearby Mecklenburg County jail.

famousMoments: Justin Thomas won the 2017 PGA Championship at Quail Hollow, his first major title, finishing at -8
Rory McIlroy made a dramatic eagle on the 18th hole to win the 2015 Wells Fargo Championship, showcasing the Green Mile's capacity for drama
Tiger Woods won the Wachovia Championship at Quail Hollow in 2007, one of 82 career PGA Tour victories
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A 195-yard par 3 in the heart of the 'Green Mile,' with water fronting the green and thick rough surrounding it. This penultimate hole has ruined countless final-round charges and is one of the most feared par 3s on the PGA Tour.
reveal.description: Quail Hollow Club in Charlotte, North Carolina, has risen from a respected PGA Tour stop to a bona fide major championship venue. Originally designed by George Cobb and later renovated by Tom Fazio, the course is defined by its fearsome 'Green Mile' closing stretch—three brutally difficult finishing holes that have produced some of the PGA Tour's most dramatic conclusions.

clues.tournamentHint: Where Francis Ouimet's 1913 U.S. Open victory as an amateur changed golf forever, and site of the 1999 Ryder Cup
clues.didYouKnow: When 20-year-old amateur Francis Ouimet defeated British legends Harry Vardon and Ted Ray in a playoff at the 1913 U.S. Open, he lived in a house directly across the street from the 17th hole. His caddie, Eddie Lowery, was just 10 years old.

famousMoments: Francis Ouimet, a 20-year-old amateur who lived across the street, defeated Harry Vardon and Ted Ray in a playoff to win the 1913 U.S. Open—a victory that democratized golf in America
Justin Leonard holed a 45-foot birdie putt on the 17th hole during the 1999 Ryder Cup, sparking a premature celebration that became one of the most iconic and controversial moments in team golf
Matt Fitzpatrick won the 2022 U.S. Open at The Country Club, 9 years after winning the 2013 U.S. Amateur on the same course
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A 375-yard par 4 where two of golf's most famous moments occurred—Francis Ouimet's 1913 U.S. Open triumph and Justin Leonard's legendary 45-foot putt during the 1999 Ryder Cup. The hole doglegs right with a creek crossing and a small, well-bunkered green.
reveal.description: The Country Club in Brookline, Massachusetts, is one of the founding clubs of the USGA and one of the most historically significant venues in American golf. Its composite course, which weaves together holes from multiple nines, has been the setting for transformative moments in the sport, from Francis Ouimet's 1913 upset to the electric 1999 Ryder Cup comeback.

clues.tournamentHint: Has hosted six U.S. Opens, three PGA Championships, and a Ryder Cup
clues.didYouKnow: Ben Hogan famously called it 'The Monster' after his legendary final-round 67 to win the 1951 U.S. Open, and the nickname has stuck ever since

famousMoments: Ben Hogan shot a final-round 67 to win the 1951 U.S. Open, declaring he had brought 'The Monster' to its knees
The U.S. team rallied from behind to reclaim the Ryder Cup at Oakland Hills in 2004
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A demanding 409-yard par 4 with a pond guarding the left side of the green and deep bunkers right, one of the toughest holes in the storied closing stretch
reveal.description: Oakland Hills Country Club, known worldwide as 'The Monster,' is a Donald Ross masterpiece later toughened by Robert Trent Jones Sr. for the 1951 U.S. Open. The South Course in suburban Detroit has hosted more major championships than nearly any other American venue and remains one of the most revered tests in golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Has hosted four U.S. Opens and two PGA Championships
clues.didYouKnow: The 1920 U.S. Open at This course was the first where professional golfers were invited into the clubhouse, a watershed moment for the sport

famousMoments: Bob Tway holed a bunker shot on the 72nd hole to stun Greg Norman and win the 1986 PGA Championship
Ted Ray won the 1920 U.S. Open, the last foreign-born winner for 50 years until Tony Jacklin in 1970
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 354-yard par 4 with a severely elevated green guarded by deep bunkers, the site of Bob Tway's iconic bunker hole-out in 1986
reveal.description: Inverness Club is a distinguished Donald Ross design in Toledo, Ohio, steeped in championship history dating back to 1920. The club broke social barriers by welcoming professional golfers into its clubhouse and has since hosted some of the most dramatic finishes in major championship golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted its first U.S. Open in 2023 after decades as one of golf's most exclusive and secretive clubs
clues.didYouKnow: The club sits on some of the most valuable real estate in the world along Wilshire Boulevard in Beverly Hills, and for decades refused to host professional tournaments

famousMoments: Wyndham Clark held off Rory McIlroy to win the 2023 U.S. Open in the club's first-ever major championship
The stunning North Course was revealed to the public for the first time during the 2023 U.S. Open broadcast
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 11: A breathtaking 236-yard par 3 playing over a massive barranca to an angled green, it became the defining image of the 2023 U.S. Open
reveal.description: Los Angeles Country Club is a George C. Thomas Jr. masterpiece that spent nearly a century as one of golf's most private and exclusive venues. The North Course, nestled in the heart of Beverly Hills, finally opened its doors to the world with the 2023 U.S. Open, revealing a spectacular Golden Age layout with dramatic barrancas and bold strategic design.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the U.S. Open in 1928 and again in 2003
clues.didYouKnow: At its peak in the 1920s, This course had four 18-hole courses and was the largest golf complex in North America

famousMoments: Jim Furyk won the 2003 U.S. Open with a steady final round on the demanding North Course
The club once boasted four championship courses, making it the largest golf facility in the country during the Roaring Twenties
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: A 448-yard par 4 with a creek crossing the fairway and a well-bunkered, elevated green, demanding two precise shots
reveal.description: Olympia Fields Country Club is a historic Willie Park Jr. design south of Chicago that once featured four full-length courses. The North Course has hosted two U.S. Opens and multiple PGA Tour events, offering a classic parkland test with mature trees, tight fairways, and demanding greens.

clues.tournamentHint: Site of John Daly's Cinderella story at the 1991 PGA Championship
clues.didYouKnow: John Daly was the 9th alternate for the 1991 PGA Championship and drove through the night from Memphis to arrive at the course, then won the tournament without a single practice round

famousMoments: John Daly, as the 9th alternate with no practice round, bombed his way to victory at the 1991 PGA Championship
The course was one of Pete Dye's earliest major designs and helped establish his reputation as a bold, unconventional architect
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: A 468-yard par 4 with a dramatic forced carry over wetlands from the tee, featuring Dye's trademark railroad-tie bulkheads along the water's edge
reveal.description: Crooked Stick Golf Club is one of Pete Dye's earliest and most celebrated designs, winding through the rolling terrain north of Indianapolis. Forever linked to John Daly's improbable 1991 PGA Championship victory, the course features Dye's signature railroad ties, bold bunkering, and strategic water hazards across a sprawling layout.

clues.tournamentHint: Annual host of the Genesis Invitational and has hosted a U.S. Open and PGA Championship
clues.didYouKnow: The course's 6th hole is one of the most unique par 3s in golf, featuring a bunker placed directly in the center of the putting green

famousMoments: Ben Hogan won three consecutive tournaments at Riviera—the 1947 and 1948 LA Opens and the 1948 U.S. Open—earning it the nickname 'Hogan's Alley'
Tiger Woods hosted and dominated the Genesis Invitational but famously never won at Riviera despite numerous attempts
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 6: A 176-yard par 3 with the iconic bunker placed directly in the center of the green, forcing players to decide whether to attack the pin or play safely around the sand
reveal.description: Riviera Country Club is a Golden Age gem designed by George C. Thomas Jr. in the Santa Monica Canyon. Known as 'Hogan's Alley' for Ben Hogan's dominance here, the course features some of the most creative and strategic holes in American golf, including the famous par-3 6th with its bunker in the middle of the green.

clues.tournamentHint: Annual home of the RBC Heritage, played the week after the Masters
clues.didYouKnow: The iconic red-and-white striped lighthouse behind the 18th green is one of the most recognizable landmarks in all of golf

famousMoments: Arnold Palmer won the inaugural Heritage Classic in 1969, giving Pete Dye's revolutionary design instant credibility
Davis Love III won the Heritage five times, becoming the tournament's all-time winningest player
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 472-yard par 4 hugging the Calibogue Sound with the iconic red-and-white lighthouse standing sentinel behind the green, providing one of golf's most photographed finishing holes
reveal.description: Harbour Town Golf Links is a Pete Dye and Jack Nicklaus collaboration on Hilton Head Island that revolutionized golf course design with its tight fairways, small greens, and strategic demands. The course rewards precision over power and is crowned by the iconic lighthouse behind the 18th green, one of the most recognizable images in golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Arnold Palmer Invitational, one of the PGA Tour's signature events
clues.didYouKnow: Arnold Palmer first played the course in 1965 and loved it so much he bought the club in 1970, making it his winter home for the rest of his life

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the Arnold Palmer Invitational a record eight times, including an iconic six-iron into the water on 18 in 2008 that he still converted into a par
Arnold Palmer hosted and shaped the tournament at his beloved Bay Hill for over four decades until his passing in 2016
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 458-yard par 4 with a large lake running the entire length of the right side and guarding the green, demanding a bold approach shot over water to a peninsula green
reveal.description: Bay Hill Club and Lodge is Arnold Palmer's personal course in Orlando, a Dick Wilson design that the King purchased in 1970 and transformed into one of the PGA Tour's most beloved annual stops. The demanding finishing stretch along the lakes of central Florida provides some of the Tour's most dramatic moments each spring.

clues.tournamentHint: Permanent home of the PGA Tour's season-ending Tour Championship
clues.didYouKnow: This course is where Bobby Jones learned to play golf as a child, growing up in a house adjacent to the course that would produce the greatest amateur golfer of all time

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the 2018 Tour Championship at East Lake for his 80th PGA Tour victory, completing one of the greatest comebacks in sports history after multiple back surgeries
Bobby Jones grew up playing East Lake and honed his legendary game on these fairways before becoming the only player to complete the Grand Slam
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 475-yard par 4 wrapping around East Lake with water down the entire left side, providing a dramatic amphitheater finish for the Tour Championship
reveal.description: East Lake Golf Club is the historic home course of Bobby Jones and the permanent venue for the PGA Tour's season-ending Tour Championship. Originally designed by Tom Bendelow and refined by Donald Ross, the course was restored by Rees Jones and sits as a cornerstone of Atlanta's golfing heritage and community revitalization.

clues.tournamentHint: Annual host of the Memorial Tournament, Jack Nicklaus's prestigious invitational
clues.didYouKnow: Jack Nicklaus named the club after the course in Scotland, where he won his first Open Championship in 1966, considering it the finest course he had ever played

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the Memorial Tournament a record five times, including a dominant wire-to-wire victory in 2012
The United States won the 1987 Ryder Cup at Muirfield Village, the first time Europe had ever competed on Jack Nicklaus-designed course
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 12: A 184-yard par 3 playing over a creek to a shallow green backed by Nicklaus's meticulous bunkering, a hole that demands precision under tournament pressure
reveal.description: Muirfield Village Golf Club is Jack Nicklaus's crown jewel, a course he designed and continually refines in his hometown of Dublin, Ohio. Home to the prestigious Memorial Tournament since 1976, the layout showcases Nicklaus's design philosophy of dramatic risk-reward holes set amid the rolling terrain and mature hardwoods of central Ohio.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2020 PGA Championship and 2009 Presidents Cup
clues.didYouKnow: This course is a rare public golf course to host a major championship, sitting next to Lake Merced in San Francisco and nearly fell into disrepair before a major renovation in 2003

famousMoments: Collin Morikawa, at just 23 years old, struck a legendary driver on the par-4 16th hole to set up eagle and win the 2020 PGA Championship
Tiger Woods went 5-0 at the 2009 Presidents Cup at Harding Park, leading the U.S. team to victory
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 444-yard par 4 flanked by towering Monterey cypress trees, playing through the classic San Francisco fog to a well-protected green
reveal.description: TPC Harding Park is a historic municipal course along the shores of Lake Merced in San Francisco, originally built in 1925 and renovated into a championship-caliber venue. One of the finest public courses in the country, it hosted the 2020 PGA Championship and showcases classic Northern California golf with Monterey cypress-lined fairways and coastal conditions.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Charles Schwab Challenge, one of the PGA Tour's oldest and most prestigious invitationals
clues.didYouKnow: This course is known as 'Hogan's Alley' because Ben Hogan won the course National Invitation Tournament five times and grew up in nearby Dublin, Texas

famousMoments: Ben Hogan won the Colonial National Invitation five times, forever linking his name to the course known as 'Hogan's Alley'
The 'Wall of Champions' along the 18th fairway displays the handprints of every Colonial champion, one of golf's most unique traditions
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 5: A 470-yard par 4 along the Trinity River, the most demanding hole in the 'Horrible Horseshoe' stretch of holes 3-5 that has defined Colonial's championship character
reveal.description: Colonial Country Club in Fort Worth is 'Hogan's Alley,' a Perry Maxwell design along the winding Trinity River that demands shotmaking precision over raw power. Home to one of the PGA Tour's longest-running events since 1946, the course is defined by its tight tree-lined fairways, small sloping greens, and the infamous 'Horrible Horseshoe' stretch.

clues.architectHint: Bert Way, redesigned by Robert Trent Jones Sr.
clues.tournamentHint: Longtime home of the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational and has hosted three PGA Championships
clues.didYouKnow: Tiger Woods won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational at the course a record eight times, completely dominating the South Course over two decades

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational eight times at Firestone, including a 15-under-par victory in 2000
Arnold Palmer made a dramatic run at the 1960 PGA Championship before Jay Hebert held on to win
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: 'The Monster,' a 667-yard par 5 that is one of the longest and most feared holes in tournament golf, with a pond guarding the green that has swallowed countless championship hopes
reveal.description: Firestone Country Club's South Course in Akron is a Robert Trent Jones Sr. redesign that became synonymous with world-class championship golf. The course hosted the WGC-Bridgestone Invitational for two decades and features the legendary 16th hole, 'The Monster,' one of the most iconic par 5s in golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Annual host of the Travelers Championship on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: Jim Furyk shot a 58 at the course in 2016, the lowest single round in PGA Tour history

famousMoments: Jim Furyk shot a record-setting 58 during the final round of the 2016 Travelers Championship, the lowest score in PGA Tour history
Bubba Watson won the Travelers Championship three times, making it one of his most successful venues
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: A driveable 296-yard par 4 perched above the Connecticut River, tempting players to go for the green while water and bunkers punish aggressive misses
reveal.description: TPC River Highlands is a Pete Dye design along the banks of the Connecticut River in Cromwell, hosting the Travelers Championship each summer. Despite being one of the shortest courses on the PGA Tour, it produces exciting finishes with its reachable par 4s and risk-reward closing stretch that has yielded the lowest round in Tour history.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Shriners Children's Open on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: This course sits in the master-planned community of the course at the base of the Spring Mountains and consistently produces some of the lowest scores on the PGA Tour

famousMoments: Tom Kim won back-to-back Shriners Children's Opens in 2022 and 2023, establishing himself as a rising star on the PGA Tour
Jim Furyk set the course record and Kevin Na made a walk-off eagle to win the 2019 Shriners Hospitals for Children Open
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A 215-yard par 3 with a green fronted by a large pond and surrounded by desert landscaping, providing a pivotal late-round test in the desert heat
reveal.description: TPC Summerlin is a Bobby Weed design nestled in the upscale Summerlin community on the western edge of Las Vegas. The desert layout offers dramatic mountain backdrops and consistently produces birdie-fests on the PGA Tour, making it one of the most exciting venues on the fall schedule.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Wyndham Championship, the final event of the PGA Tour regular season
clues.didYouKnow: The Wyndham Championship at This course is the PGA Tour's fourth-oldest event, dating back to 1938 as the Greater Greensboro Open

famousMoments: Sam Snead won the Greater Greensboro Open a record eight times, cementing his connection to this classic Donald Ross course
The tournament serves as the final regular-season event, often producing dramatic finishes as players battle to qualify for the FedExCup Playoffs
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 12: A 387-yard par 4 with a classic Donald Ross crowned green set above a pond, demanding a precise approach to hold the putting surface
reveal.description: Sedgefield Country Club is a beautifully preserved Donald Ross design in Greensboro, North Carolina, hosting one of the PGA Tour's oldest events. The compact, strategic layout rewards precision and creativity over brute power, with Ross's trademark crowned greens and subtle mounding providing a classic championship test.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the FedEx St. Jude Championship, a premier FedExCup Playoffs event
clues.didYouKnow: This course was elevated from a regular PGA Tour event to a World Golf Championship and then to a FedExCup Playoff event, one of the biggest jumps in status for any Tour venue

famousMoments: Justin Thomas won the WGC-FedEx St. Jude Invitational in 2020, solidifying TPC Southwind's status as a world-class championship venue
The course's island-green 11th hole has produced dramatic moments reminiscent of TPC Sawgrass's famous 17th
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 11: A 163-yard par 3 to a near-island green surrounded by water on three sides, demanding a precise tee shot under intense playoff pressure
reveal.description: TPC Southwind is a Ron Prichard design in Memphis that has risen from a regular PGA Tour stop to one of the most important venues in the FedExCup Playoffs. The tree-lined layout features strategic water hazards, most notably the island-green par-3 11th, and provides a stern test for the world's best players.

clues.tournamentHint: A newer PGA Tour venue that hosted the CJ Cup and the Palmetto Championship
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built with a social mission—the course Foundation provides free golf instruction, academic tutoring, and mentorship to underserved youth in the South Carolina Lowcountry

famousMoments: Garrick Higgo won the 2021 Palmetto Championship at Congaree in just his second PGA Tour start, announcing himself on the world stage
Rory McIlroy won the 2022 CJ Cup at Congaree, praising the Tom Fazio layout as one of the best modern designs he had played
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 3: A sweeping 598-yard par 5 through the Lowcountry landscape with sandy waste areas and a green set among live oaks, rewarding bold play off the tee
reveal.description: Congaree Golf Club is a Tom Fazio design in the South Carolina Lowcountry built on a former rice plantation, featuring wide fairways, sandy waste areas, and minimalist bunkering that evokes the region's natural beauty. Beyond its championship caliber, the club is driven by a charitable mission to provide opportunities for underserved youth through golf and education.

clues.tournamentHint: Part of The American Express tournament rotation in the California desert
clues.didYouKnow: Lee Trevino called The course 'Star Wars golf' when it opened because of Pete Dye's extreme design features, including the famous island-green par 3 nicknamed 'Alcatraz'

famousMoments: Lee Trevino dubbed the course 'Star Wars golf' upon its opening in 1986, stunned by Pete Dye's severe design features
The 'Alcatraz' 17th hole has claimed countless balls and provided dramatic moments during The American Express tournament
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: 'Alcatraz,' a 168-yard par 3 to a tiny island green surrounded entirely by water and rocks, one of the most intimidating shots in all of golf
reveal.description: PGA West's Stadium Course is Pete Dye at his most diabolical, set against the dramatic backdrop of the Santa Rosa Mountains in the California desert. Known for extreme features like the island-green 'Alcatraz' 17th hole and deep pot bunkers, the course pushes the boundaries of golf architecture and has become a bucket-list destination for adventurous golfers.

clues.tournamentHint: Former home of the Western Open and BMW Championship on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: The course is a public course nicknamed 'Dubsdread' because it was designed to be so difficult that even scratch golfers would 'dread' playing it

famousMoments: Tiger Woods won the BMW Championship at Cog Hill in 2007 and 2009, dominating the Dubsdread course with his elite ball-striking
The Western Open at Cog Hill was one of the PGA Tour's most prestigious events for over four decades before being rebranded as the BMW Championship
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 467-yard par 4 with a demanding uphill approach to a well-bunkered green, providing a grueling finish to one of the toughest public courses in America
reveal.description: Cog Hill's Dubsdread No. 4 is one of the most famous public courses in America, a Dick Wilson and Joe Lee design that hosted the Western Open for decades and later the BMW Championship. Renovated by Rees Jones in 2008, the course offers a championship-caliber challenge accessible to everyday golfers in the southwestern suburbs of Chicago.

clues.tournamentHint: Has hosted multiple FedExCup Playoff events and the 2017 Presidents Cup
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built on a former landfill and toxic waste site on the Jersey City waterfront, with stunning views of the Statue of the course, Ellis Island, and the Manhattan skyline

famousMoments: The U.S. team dominated the 2017 Presidents Cup at Liberty National, with the Statue of Liberty providing a patriotic backdrop for the international competition
Patrick Reed's controversial victory at the 2019 Northern Trust showcased the course's dramatic finishing holes with the Manhattan skyline in the background
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 14: A 253-yard par 3 with panoramic views of the Statue of Liberty and the Manhattan skyline, blending world-class golf with one of the most iconic backdrops in sports
reveal.description: Liberty National Golf Club is a modern marvel built on reclaimed land along the Jersey City waterfront, offering jaw-dropping views of the Statue of Liberty, Ellis Island, and the Manhattan skyline. Designed by Tom Kite and Bob Cupp, the course transforms an industrial landscape into a championship venue that has hosted Presidents Cup and FedExCup Playoff events.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2021 BMW Championship, a FedExCup Playoff event
clues.didYouKnow: This course was named after the natural limestone the course and valleys found throughout the property in the rolling hills northwest of Baltimore

famousMoments: Patrick Cantlay defeated Bryson DeChambeau in a thrilling six-hole playoff to win the 2021 BMW Championship, one of the most dramatic finishes in FedExCup Playoff history
The 2021 BMW Championship showcased Caves Valley's championship pedigree and cemented its place among elite PGA Tour venues
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 559-yard par 5 sweeping downhill with a creek crossing in front of the green, offering an amphitheater setting and eagle opportunity for a dramatic finish
reveal.description: Caves Valley Golf Club is a Tom Fazio design carved through the rolling, wooded terrain of Maryland's Greenspring Valley northwest of Baltimore. The spacious layout features dramatic elevation changes, strategic water hazards, and Fazio's signature immaculate conditioning, providing a world-class championship experience in the Mid-Atlantic.

clues.tournamentHint: Current home of the CJ Cup Byron Nelson on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: This course became the Byron Nelson's new home in 2021 after the tournament moved from Trinity Forest and the TPC Four Seasons, marking a new chapter for one of the Tour's storied events

famousMoments: K.H. Lee won back-to-back Byron Nelson titles in 2021 and 2022, the first player to defend the title successfully in the tournament's modern era
The move to TPC Craig Ranch reinvigorated the Byron Nelson tournament with a modern Tom Weiskopf design north of Dallas
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 14: A 603-yard par 5 with a strategic creek winding through the fairway, offering multiple routes and a risk-reward decision on whether to go for the green in two
reveal.description: TPC Craig Ranch is a Tom Weiskopf design in McKinney, Texas, that became the new home of the Byron Nelson tournament in 2021. The North Texas layout features wide fairways, strategic water features, and Weiskopf's naturalistic design philosophy, providing an accessible yet challenging championship venue in the rapidly growing Dallas suburbs.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the John the course Classic on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: Jordan Spieth won the 2013 John the course Classic at age 19 on a sponsor's exemption, becoming the first teenager to win on the PGA Tour in 82 years

famousMoments: Jordan Spieth won the 2013 John Deere Classic at age 19, holing a bunker shot on the 72nd hole to force a playoff and then winning on the fifth extra hole
Steve Stricker won the John Deere Classic three consecutive years (2009-2011), dominating the event at TPC Deere Run
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A 454-yard par 4 running alongside the Rock River, with water guarding the left side of the green and a natural amphitheater setting for the tournament's dramatic finishes
reveal.description: TPC Deere Run is a D.A. Weibring design along the Rock River in the Quad Cities, purpose-built to host the John Deere Classic. The accessible public layout has become a launching pad for future stars, most notably Jordan Spieth, and features gently rolling Midwestern terrain with strategic water hazards and a scenic riverside finish.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Fortinet Championship, the PGA Tour's fall season opener
clues.didYouKnow: Johnny Miller, who grew up nearby and became one of golf's greatest players, personally oversaw a major renovation of the North Course to bring it up to PGA Tour standards

famousMoments: The Fortinet Championship at Silverado kicks off the new PGA Tour season each fall, set among the vineyards and rolling hills of Napa Valley
Johnny Miller, a Napa Valley native and World Golf Hall of Famer, redesigned the course and served as the tournament's ambassador for years
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 11: A 387-yard par 4 flanked by towering oaks and bordered by a creek, with a demanding approach to a green framed by Napa Valley's wine country landscape
reveal.description: Silverado Resort's North Course is a Robert Trent Jones Jr. design set amid the rolling vineyards of Napa Valley, serving as the PGA Tour's fall season opener. Renovated by local legend Johnny Miller, the tree-lined layout weaves through the wine country landscape and offers a uniquely Californian setting for championship golf.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional tournaments, but home to numerous USGA amateur events
clues.didYouKnow: This course was the first course built at the resort and was designed by a then-unknown 26-year-old Scottish architect, David McLay Kidd, who was given almost complete creative freedom by founder Mike Keiser.

famousMoments: Mike Keiser's bold vision to build a remote links resort on the Oregon coast became one of the most celebrated golf destinations in the world
David McLay Kidd's breakout design put him on the map as a world-class architect
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A dramatic par-4 of 363 yards that plays along the edge of the bluff with the Pacific Ocean crashing below, requiring a precise tee shot and an approach to a green perched above the sea.
reveal.description: The original course at Bandon Dunes Golf Resort, this links-style layout hugs the rugged Oregon coastline with sweeping ocean views. Designed by David McLay Kidd, the course features firm, fast conditions and windswept terrain that evoke the great links of Scotland and Ireland.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; consistently ranked as the top public-access course in America
clues.didYouKnow: This course features more oceanfront holes than any other course at the resort, with eleven holes offering direct views of the course, and Tom Doak considers it one of his finest designs.

famousMoments: Immediately upon opening, Pacific Dunes vaulted into the top 10 of nearly every major course ranking list
Tom Doak famously routed the course to maximize ocean exposure, using minimal earth-moving to let the natural dunes dictate the design
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 13: A breathtaking par-3 of 181 yards played from an elevated tee to a green set against the Pacific Ocean, with bunkers guarding the front and a steep drop-off behind.
reveal.description: Often regarded as the crown jewel of Bandon Dunes Golf Resort, Pacific Dunes is a masterpiece of minimalist design by Tom Doak. The course weaves through towering sand dunes and along dramatic oceanside cliffs, delivering a pure links experience that rivals the best courses in the British Isles.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional tournaments; widely recognized as a top resort destination
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built on a former phosphate mining site in central Florida, and the massive sand dunes left behind by decades of mining created terrain that looks more like the Sand Hills of Nebraska than the Sunshine State.

famousMoments: The Red Course debuted at No. 1 on Golf Magazine's Best New Courses list in 2013
The site's transformation from industrial wasteland to world-class golf destination is considered one of the most remarkable reclamation stories in golf
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A stunning par-3 of 230 yards with a massive waste bunker fronting the green and sand dunes framing both sides, demanding a precise long iron or hybrid into the prevailing wind.
reveal.description: Designed by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw, the Red Course at Streamsong Resort is carved through towering sand ridges in the heart of central Florida. The layout features dramatic elevation changes, enormous bunkers, and wide fairways that encourage creative shot-making in a landscape unlike anything else in the state.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; quickly became one of the highest-ranked public courses in America
clues.didYouKnow: This course sits atop an ancient glacial lake bed, and the sandy soil is so pure that the resort's founders discovered they could build world-class golf on terrain that had been used for decades as a tree farm.

famousMoments: Mike Keiser, the visionary behind Bandon Dunes, partnered with his sons to create Sand Valley as the Midwest's answer to Bandon
The resort's Sandbox — a massive 17-hole par-3 course designed by Bill Coore — became an instant cult favorite
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A dramatic par-3 of 170 yards played from an elevated tee down to a green surrounded by natural sand blowouts, with sweeping views of the Wisconsin pines.
reveal.description: Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw crafted Sand Valley through the glacial sand deposits of central Wisconsin, creating a course that feels transported from the British Isles. The gently rolling terrain, natural sand features, and fescue-lined fairways provide a walking-only experience that celebrates the simplicity and beauty of minimalist golf design.

clues.tournamentHint: Part of the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am rotation
clues.didYouKnow: This course is named after locations in Robert Louis Stevenson's 'Treasure Island,' and each hole carries a name from the novel, as Stevenson once wandered the Monterey Peninsula's forests and dunes.

famousMoments: Jack Nicklaus once called the first five holes at Spyglass Hill the greatest stretch of opening holes in golf
The course consistently plays as the toughest in the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am rotation
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 1: A downhill par-5 of 595 yards that plunges through sand dunes toward the ocean, offering panoramic views of the Pacific and a dramatic introduction to the course.
reveal.description: Spyglass Hill weaves from stunning oceanside dunes into the dense Del Monte Forest on California's Monterey Peninsula. Designed by Robert Trent Jones Sr., the front nine features links-style holes along the coast while the back nine winds through towering Monterey pines, creating one of golf's most dramatically varied 18-hole experiences.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the CJ Cup and a high-profile Tiger vs. Phil match
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built in the flat Nevada desert at a reported cost of over $60 million by Steve Wynn, with Tom Fazio moving 21,000 trees and reshaping the barren terrain into a lush, rolling landscape that feels like the Carolinas.

famousMoments: Phil Mickelson defeated Tiger Woods in 'The Match' at Shadow Creek in 2018, winning $9 million in a pay-per-view event watched by millions
Steve Wynn reportedly spent over $60 million to transform flat desert into one of the most exclusive courses in the world
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: A beautiful par-3 of 175 yards over water to an island-like green, surrounded by mature trees and flowering landscaping that seems impossible in the Nevada desert.
reveal.description: Shadow Creek is a man-made masterpiece carved from the barren Nevada desert north of the Las Vegas Strip. Tom Fazio sculpted rolling hills, planted thousands of trees, and created streams and waterfalls to produce a course that feels like it belongs in the Appalachian foothills rather than the arid Southwest.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; celebrated as one of the top public courses in the Midwest
clues.didYouKnow: This course sits 200 feet above Lake Michigan on dramatic coastal the course, and the course was built with fescue grasses imported from Europe to create an authentic links-like playing surface.

famousMoments: The course opened to immediate critical acclaim, drawing comparisons to famous Irish links courses like Ballybunion
The addition of The South Course, designed by Dana Fry, gave the resort a second world-class layout in 2018
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 12: A stunning par-3 of 185 yards played along the bluff's edge with Lake Michigan stretching out to the horizon, requiring a precise shot to a green backed by a steep drop-off.
reveal.description: Perched high on bluffs overlooking Lake Michigan, Arcadia Bluffs is a links-style course that captures the wild beauty of the Irish coastline in northern Michigan. The exposed layout features firm fescue fairways, deep pot bunkers, and relentless wind off the lake that demands creative shot-making and imagination.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted numerous USGA amateur championships
clues.didYouKnow: Alister MacKenzie loved the course so much that he built his home overlooking the 6th fairway and lived there until his death in 1934 — it was the last course he completed in America.

notableTournaments: NCAA Division I Women's Golf Championship
famousMoments: Alister MacKenzie chose to live out his final years in a home adjacent to the 6th hole, considering Pasatiempo his finest work in California
Tom Doak led a meticulous restoration of the course in 2007, recovering many of MacKenzie's original bunker shapes and green contours
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A world-class par-4 of 395 yards featuring a deep barranca crossing the fairway and a dramatically contoured green that MacKenzie considered one of his best.
reveal.description: Designed by the legendary Alister MacKenzie, Pasatiempo is a golden-age gem set in the rolling hills above Santa Cruz, California. The course features MacKenzie's trademark bold bunkering, inventive green complexes, and strategic angles that reward thoughtful play over brute force.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; debuted alongside the Red Course as one of the best new courses in the country
clues.didYouKnow: Tom Doak and Coore & Crenshaw were given adjacent parcels on the old phosphate mine and told to build simultaneously — each architect could see the other's work taking shape across the ridgeline.

famousMoments: The Blue Course opened alongside the Red Course in 2012, giving Streamsong an instant one-two punch that rivaled any resort in America
Tom Doak utilized the tallest sand ridges on the property, creating more dramatic elevation changes than typically found anywhere in Florida
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 7: A thrilling par-3 of 207 yards played from atop a massive sand ridge down to a green nestled between dunes, with a cavernous bunker short and a steep falloff behind.
reveal.description: Tom Doak's Blue Course at Streamsong Resort showcases his minimalist philosophy amid the dramatic sand ridges of a former phosphate mine in central Florida. The layout features bold green sites, sweeping vistas across the untouched landscape, and a rugged beauty that defies every stereotype about Florida golf.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; widely regarded as one of the best public courses in Arizona
clues.didYouKnow: This course is owned by the Fort McDowell Yavapai Nation, and the layout was routed through pristine Sonoran Desert that had never been disturbed, with Coore & Crenshaw moving remarkably little earth.

famousMoments: Bill Coore called the property one of the finest natural sites he had ever been given for a golf course
The course instantly became the top-rated public course in Arizona upon opening
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 2: A gorgeous par-4 of 440 yards framed by towering saguaro cacti, with a split fairway that offers two distinct angles into a green set against the McDowell Mountains.
reveal.description: Set in the unspoiled Sonoran Desert northeast of Scottsdale, the Saguaro Course at We Ko Pa is a stunning Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw design that seamlessly integrates with its natural surroundings. Towering saguaro cacti, rugged desert washes, and panoramic mountain views frame wide fairways and creative green complexes.

clues.tournamentHint: Former AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am rotation course; hosted Walker Cup
clues.didYouKnow: This course withdrew from the AT&T Pebble Beach Pro-Am rotation in 1991 after refusing to comply with PGA Tour requirements regarding membership policies, and was replaced by Poppy Hills and later Spyglass Hill.

famousMoments: Tom Watson holed a remarkable shot on the iconic 16th hole during the 1981 Bing Crosby Pro-Am
In the 1981 Walker Cup, the U.S. defeated Great Britain & Ireland at Cypress Point in a memorable match
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: Perhaps the most famous par-3 in golf, this 231-yard hole requires a carry entirely over the Pacific Ocean to a green perched on a rocky promontory, with crashing waves below.
reveal.description: Cypress Point Club is widely considered one of the greatest golf courses ever built, a masterpiece by Alister MacKenzie on California's Monterey Peninsula. The course transitions from forested inland holes to breathtaking oceanside stretches, culminating in the legendary 15th, 16th, and 17th holes along the rugged Pacific coastline.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the Walker Cup and Crump Cup invitational
clues.didYouKnow: This course has been ranked the No. 1 golf course in the world by nearly every major publication for decades, yet founder George Crump died in 1918 before seeing his masterpiece fully completed.

famousMoments: John Arthur Brown is the only player known to have shot under par in his first round at Pine Valley, carding a 68 in 1940
The club's legendary difficulty is so renowned that countless golfers have famously come undone on individual holes, with the 7th hole in particular earning a fearsome reputation
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 7: A devilish par-5 of 630 yards known as 'Hell's Half Acre,' featuring an enormous sandy waste area in the middle of the fairway that has destroyed countless scorecards.
reveal.description: Widely regarded as the No. 1 golf course in the world, Pine Valley is a penal masterpiece set among the sandy pine barrens of southern New Jersey. George Crump's vision, refined by H.S. Colt, created a course where every shot must be precisely executed — heroic carries over vast sandy wastelands connect island-like fairways and greens.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the Walker Cup and early USGA championships
clues.didYouKnow: Charles Blair Macdonald spent years studying the great holes of Scotland and England before building the National, creating The course's first course intentionally designed to incorporate 'template' holes like the Redan, Alps, and Eden.

famousMoments: The 2013 Walker Cup returned to the National Golf Links for the first time in 91 years, with the U.S. team winning on one of golf's most historic stages
C.B. Macdonald's template hole concept at the National influenced virtually every American golf architect who followed
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 4 (Redan): A 195-yard par-3 modeled after the famous 15th at North Berwick, featuring a green angled away from the player and sloping from front-right to back-left, defended by a deep bunker.
reveal.description: The National Golf Links of America is Charles Blair Macdonald's magnum opus, a pioneering course on the eastern end of Long Island that introduced the concept of template holes to American golf. Set on rolling terrain overlooking Peconic Bay, the course features reproductions of famous British holes adapted brilliantly to the American landscape.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the 2021 Walker Cup and The Match featuring Tiger vs. Peyton Manning and Phil vs. Tom Brady
clues.didYouKnow: Ben Hogan considered the course his favorite course and would travel to Juno Beach every spring to prepare for the Masters, playing the course daily and fine-tuning his game on its windswept layout.

famousMoments: Ben Hogan made annual spring pilgrimages to Seminole to sharpen his game before Augusta, calling it his favorite course in America
Tiger Woods, Peyton Manning, Phil Mickelson, and Tom Brady played 'The Match: Champions for Charity' at Seminole in 2020, giving the world a rare televised look at the ultra-private club
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 6: A demanding par-4 of 433 yards that runs along the Atlantic coast ridge, requiring a drive that must contend with prevailing ocean winds and an approach to one of Ross's most expertly contoured greens.
reveal.description: Seminole Golf Club is Donald Ross's Florida masterpiece, a windswept layout along the Atlantic coast in Juno Beach that is revered by purists. The course features Ross's characteristic crowned greens, strategic bunkering, and constantly shifting ocean breezes that make it play differently every day.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; consistently ranked among the top 10-20 courses in the world
clues.didYouKnow: the course' greens are considered among the most wildly contoured in all of golf — MacKenzie and Maxwell created putting surfaces with so much internal movement that even elite players can four-putt from 20 feet.

famousMoments: The course was nearly lost to financial ruin during the Great Depression, but a small group of dedicated members kept it alive
Golf historians consider Crystal Downs to be the finest example of MacKenzie and Maxwell's collaborative work in America
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 8: A spectacular par-3 of 185 yards played from an elevated tee to a green perched on a bluff with views of Crystal Lake and Lake Michigan, featuring one of MacKenzie's most audaciously contoured putting surfaces.
reveal.description: Crystal Downs is a collaborative masterpiece by Alister MacKenzie and Perry Maxwell, set on a high bluff between Crystal Lake and Lake Michigan in northern Michigan. The course is celebrated for its wildly inventive green complexes, strategic bunkering, and a natural routing that flows beautifully through rolling, windswept terrain.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the U.S. Women's Open and U.S. Senior Open
clues.didYouKnow: Perry Maxwell built only nine holes at the course before his death; his son Press Maxwell completed the back nine in 1957, seamlessly matching his father's design vision twenty years later.

famousMoments: Juli Inkster won the 2002 U.S. Women's Open at Prairie Dunes in a memorable championship
Tom Watson, a Kansas native, has called Prairie Dunes one of the finest courses in America and a hidden gem of the heartland
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 8: A challenging par-4 of 430 yards that plays through a natural valley of sand dunes and yucca plants, demanding a precise approach to a heavily contoured green protected by deep bunkers.
reveal.description: Prairie Dunes is a remarkable Perry Maxwell design set amid the sand hills and native prairie of central Kansas. The course features rolling dunes covered with yucca, plum thickets, and cottonwood trees, creating a links-like experience in the heart of the Great Plains that has earned it recognition as one of America's greatest hidden gems.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; consistently ranked among the top 10 courses in America
clues.didYouKnow: This course is so remote that the nearest commercial airport is over three hours away, and the tiny town of Mullen, Nebraska has a population of roughly 500 people.

famousMoments: Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw reportedly found 130 potential holes during their routing process, the natural terrain was so perfectly suited for golf
The course's opening in 1995 launched the minimalist design movement in American golf architecture
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 7: A stunning par-3 of 175 yards played into a natural amphitheater of grass-covered dunes, with a green nestled in a valley that appears untouched by human hands.
reveal.description: Sand Hills Golf Club is the course that launched the minimalist movement in American golf design. Built by Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw in the remote Sand Hills region of Nebraska, the course appears as if it has existed for centuries — wide fairways flow through enormous grass-covered dunes with almost no visible man-made features.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted early U.S. Opens, U.S. Amateurs, and the Walker Cup
clues.didYouKnow: This course was one of the five founding member clubs of the USGA in 1894 and hosted the first U.S. Open held at an 18-hole course in 1897.

famousMoments: The 1897 U.S. Open at Chicago Golf Club was the first played over 72 holes on an 18-hole course
The U.S. team won the 2005 Walker Cup at Chicago Golf Club, returning high-level competition to the historic venue after nearly a century
