reveal.signatureHole: Hole 14: A par-3 of 210 yards modeled after the Redan at North Berwick, featuring a long, narrow green angled diagonally to the tee with a deep bunker guarding the left front.
reveal.description: One of the five founding clubs of the USGA, Chicago Golf Club is a historic treasure designed by Charles Blair Macdonald and later refined by Seth Raynor. The flat Illinois terrain belies the course's strategic depth, with template holes inspired by the great links of Britain and firm, fast conditions that reward precision and creativity.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; accessible only by ferry or private boat
clues.didYouKnow: This course is accessible only by ferry from New London, Connecticut, and Seth Raynor died just a few months before the course opened, never seeing his seaside masterpiece in its finished form.

famousMoments: Seth Raynor passed away in January 1926, months before his greatest work at Fishers Island was completed
The club has remained virtually unchanged since opening, preserving Raynor's original template designs in near-perfect condition
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 5: A spectacular par-3 of 229 yards known as the 'Punchbowl,' played across a cove of Fishers Island Sound to a green set in a natural bowl surrounded by dunes and ocean.
reveal.description: Seth Raynor's masterwork on a private island between Long Island Sound and the Atlantic Ocean, Fishers Island Club is a stunning seaside course that seamlessly blends classic template holes with dramatic coastal terrain. The course features Raynor's signature Redan, Biarritz, and Alps holes set against a backdrop of crashing waves and sweeping ocean panoramas.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; considered one of the top 50 courses in America
clues.didYouKnow: This course was built for just 35 members and has maintained an extremely small, exclusive membership ever since — it's named after a Spanish explorer who traveled through Ohio in the 16th century.

famousMoments: The club was founded by a small group of Cincinnati businessmen who wanted an intimate course inspired by the great links of Britain
Seth Raynor adapted his famous template holes to the hilly, wooded terrain of the Ohio River valley with remarkable ingenuity
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 8: A challenging par-3 of 210 yards modeled after the famous Biarritz template, featuring a distinctive swale running through the middle of a long, narrow green.
reveal.description: Camargo Club is one of Seth Raynor's finest inland designs, set in the rolling wooded hills north of Cincinnati, Ohio. The intimate course features Raynor's classic template holes brilliantly adapted to dramatic terrain, with elevation changes and mature hardwoods creating a completely different character from his more famous coastal works.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted multiple U.S. Amateurs and other USGA events
clues.didYouKnow: Walter Travis, who redesigned the course, was also a champion golfer who won three U.S. Amateurs and the 1904 British Amateur — he brought his player's eye to the bunkering and green contours that define the course today.

famousMoments: Walter Travis transformed the flat Hempstead Plains terrain with over 100 deep bunkers, creating one of the most strategically demanding courses on Long Island
The course hosted six U.S. Amateurs in the early 20th century, more than almost any other course during that era
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 9: A demanding par-4 of 455 yards that doglegs gently through deep cross bunkers, requiring two precise shots to reach a well-defended green — a quintessential Travis strategic hole.
reveal.description: Garden City Golf Club is a historic masterpiece on the flat Hempstead Plains of Long Island, transformed by Walter Travis's genius bunkering from ordinary terrain into an extraordinary strategic test. The over 100 deep bunkers, firm turf, and windswept conditions create a links-like challenge just 20 miles from Manhattan.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosted the BMW Championship and PGA Championship
clues.didYouKnow: Donald Ross reportedly called the course his 'finest work,' and the club has undergone a painstaking restoration led by Gil Hanse to return the course to Ross's original design intent.

famousMoments: Keegan Bradley won the 2018 BMW Championship at Aronimink with a dominant 20-under par performance
Gary Player won the 1962 PGA Championship at Aronimink, part of his career Grand Slam
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 9: A classic Donald Ross par-4 of 465 yards that sweeps gently uphill to a crowned green with Ross's trademark false front and subtle tiers that make distance control critical.
reveal.description: Aronimink Golf Club is a Donald Ross masterpiece in the Philadelphia suburbs, featuring the architect's finest strategic bunkering and his hallmark crowned green complexes. Ross reportedly called it his greatest work, and a meticulous Gil Hanse restoration has returned the course to its original glory.

clues.tournamentHint: No major professional events; rocketed into the top 100 immediately upon opening
clues.didYouKnow: This course features an incredibly diverse routing that starts in dense forest, moves through open meadows, reaches oceanside bluffs overlooking Long Island Sound, and returns through rolling dunes — all in 18 holes.

famousMoments: Friar's Head debuted inside the top 50 on most major ranking lists, one of the fastest ascents for any new course
Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw were given an extraordinary property with five distinct types of terrain, and they routed the course to showcase each one
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: A dramatic par-3 of 200 yards played from high atop a bluff down to a green near the shore of Long Island Sound, with sweeping water views and wind that can change the club selection by three or four clubs.
reveal.description: Friar's Head is a Bill Coore and Ben Crenshaw design on Long Island's North Shore that traverses an astonishing variety of landscapes in a single round. The course journeys from dense forest to open meadows to oceanside bluffs, delivering one of the most visually dramatic and strategically engaging experiences in American golf.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Zurich Classic of New Orleans, the PGA Tour's only team event
clues.didYouKnow: This course is built on wetlands along the Mississippi River, and the course was engineered with an elaborate drainage system to manage the low-lying terrain — alligators are frequent visitors on the property.

famousMoments: The Zurich Classic became the PGA Tour's first and only official team event in 2017, bringing a unique format to TPC Louisiana
Billy Horschel and Sam Burns won the 2023 Zurich Classic in a dominant team performance
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A risk-reward par-5 of 555 yards that wraps around a large lake, tempting long hitters to cut the corner and go for the green in two while water threatens the entire left side.
reveal.description: TPC Louisiana is a Pete Dye design built in the bayou wetlands west of New Orleans, serving as the permanent home of the Zurich Classic. The course features Dye's trademark railroad-tie bulkheads, strategic water hazards, and a challenging layout carved through cypress-lined corridors and Louisiana marshland.

clues.tournamentHint: Former home of the PGA Tour's 'A Military Tribute at the course'
clues.didYouKnow: the course resort has a declassified Cold War bunker built beneath its West Virginia Wing that was designed to house the entire U.S. Congress in the event of a nuclear attack.

notableTournaments: A Military Tribute at The Greenbrier (2010-2019)
famousMoments: The 1979 Ryder Cup at The Greenbrier was the last to feature only American and British players before the format expanded to include all of Europe
Sam Snead grew up near The Greenbrier and served as the resort's golf professional emeritus for decades
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: A dramatic par-4 of 447 yards featuring a tee shot over Howard's Creek and an uphill approach to a green set against the backdrop of the iconic Greenbrier hotel.
reveal.description: The Old White TPC at The Greenbrier is a historic C.B. Macdonald and Seth Raynor design set in the Allegheny Mountains of West Virginia. The course winds through a scenic valley with mountain views, featuring classic template holes and a storied history that includes hosting the Ryder Cup and serving as the home course of legendary Sam Snead.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the RSM Classic on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: Davis Love III, who lives on St. Simons Island, serves as tournament host of the RSM Classic and helped shape the club's identity as one of the premier resort destinations in the South.

famousMoments: Robert Streb defeated Kevin Kisner in a dramatic playoff at the 2020 RSM Classic
The original Seaside Course was designed by Harry S. Colt and Charles Alison in 1929 before Tom Fazio's complete redesign in 1999
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: Par 3, 196 yards, a stunning approach over water with the historic St. Simons lighthouse framed in the background
reveal.description: Sea Island's Seaside Course, redesigned by Tom Fazio in 1999, winds through salt marshes and along the Georgia coast on St. Simons Island. The course blends classic coastal links character with modern resort golf, offering ocean breezes and panoramic views of the marshlands and lighthouse.

clues.tournamentHint: Long-time home of the Honda Classic on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: The infamous 'Bear Trap' — holes 15, 16, and 17 — was named after Jack Nicklaus who redesigned the course and is statistically one of the most difficult three-hole stretches on the entire PGA Tour.

famousMoments: Jack Nicklaus captained the U.S. Ryder Cup team to victory here in 1983
Padraig Harrington survived the Bear Trap to win the 2015 Honda Classic after a four-shot comeback on the final day
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: Par 3, 179 yards, the start of the Bear Trap, requiring a carry over water to a green surrounded by bunkers and spectator mounds
reveal.description: PGA National's Champion Course is a demanding South Florida layout originally designed by Tom and George Fazio in 1981, later redesigned by Jack Nicklaus. Home to the Honda Classic for decades, the course is defined by its punishing water hazards and the legendary Bear Trap stretch that has derailed countless PGA Tour contenders.

clues.tournamentHint: Host of the Valero Texas Open on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: Sergio Garcia served as player consultant during the design process, contributing insights that shaped several of the course's strategic angles and green complexes.

famousMoments: Jordan Spieth won the 2021 Valero Texas Open to clinch his spot at the Masters, ending a nearly four-year winless drought
Corey Conners Monday-qualified and then won the 2019 Valero Texas Open, one of the rarest feats in modern PGA Tour history
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 16: Par 3, 180 yards, featuring a dramatic downhill approach to an island-style green framed by native Texas Hill Country oaks and limestone outcroppings
reveal.description: TPC San Antonio's AT&T Oaks Course is a Greg Norman design carved through the Texas Hill Country, featuring rolling terrain, mature oak trees, and dramatic elevation changes. The course has served as the permanent home of the Valero Texas Open since 2010 and offers one of the most scenic PGA Tour stops.

clues.tournamentHint: Home of the Valspar Championship on the PGA Tour
clues.didYouKnow: The treacherous finishing stretch of holes 16-18 is known as the 'Snake Pit,' named for the course snakes that inhabit the surrounding pine forests and wetlands of the course resort.

famousMoments: Tiger Woods made his much-anticipated comeback at the 2018 Valspar Championship, finishing one shot behind winner Paul Casey in a thrilling final round
Sam Burns won back-to-back Valspar Championships in 2021 and 2022, dominating the Copperhead Course
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 17: Par 3, 215 yards, the centerpiece of the Snake Pit, with a severely contoured green tucked between towering pines and a deep bunker complex that has undone countless final-round bids
reveal.description: Innisbrook's Copperhead Course is a Larry Packard design that winds through dense stands of pine, cypress, and palm trees in the Tampa Bay area. Despite being in flat Florida, the course features surprising elevation changes and the infamous Snake Pit finishing stretch that consistently produces dramatic Valspar Championship conclusions.

clues.tournamentHint: Part of the composite course used for the 2012 Ryder Cup, also hosted the 1998 U.S. Women's Open
clues.didYouKnow: The Sheboygan River comes into play on seven of the eighteen holes, making it one of the most river-influenced championship courses in the country. Pete Dye called it one of his finest designs.

famousMoments: Se Ri Pak won the 1998 U.S. Women's Open here in a dramatic 20-hole playoff against Jenny Chuasiriporn, helping ignite Korean golf's rise to global prominence

Holes from the River Course were combined with Meadow Valleys to create the composite course for the 2012 Ryder Cup, won by Europe in the 'Miracle at Medinah' — though the miracle occurred at the following year's venue in spirit
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 11: Par 4, 364 yards, a sharp dogleg left with the Sheboygan River running the entire length of the hole, demanding a precise tee shot to a fairway perched above the river gorge
reveal.description: Blackwolf Run's River Course is a Pete Dye masterpiece set along the banks and bluffs of the Sheboygan River in Kohler, Wisconsin. Part of the Kohler resort family, the course features dramatic ravines, glacial terrain, and river crossings that create one of the most visually stunning and strategically demanding inland courses in America.

clues.tournamentHint: Has not hosted a major professional tournament; ultra-exclusive Hamptons club
clues.didYouKnow: This course was the first major course design by Rees Jones on Long Island, and its creation in the early 1990s helped spark a renaissance in high-end golf club development across the Hamptons.

famousMoments: Rees Jones crafted the course through pristine Hamptons farmland and scrub oak forest, earning the club immediate recognition as one of the finest private clubs in the Northeast
The club maintains an extremely limited membership and no tee time sheet, allowing members to play at their leisure in true old-world fashion
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 11: Par 4, 445 yards, a sweeping dogleg left through native fescue and scrub oak with a demanding approach to a well-guarded green set against the Long Island horizon
reveal.description: Atlantic Golf Club is an ultra-exclusive Rees Jones design in the heart of the Hamptons, opened in 1992. The course moves through rolling farmland, dense scrub oak, and tall fescue grasses, offering a links-like experience in one of America's most coveted zip codes.

clues.tournamentHint: Hosts the Junior Invitational at the course, a prestigious amateur event for the world's top junior golfers
clues.didYouKnow: The Junior Invitational at This course is modeled after the Masters Tournament, complete with an invite-only field, and has featured future PGA Tour stars including Jordan Spieth, Justin Thomas, and Joaquin Niemann as past participants.

famousMoments: Jordan Spieth competed in the Junior Invitational years before becoming a Masters champion, showcasing the event's role as a proving ground for future stars
The club was founded just across the Savannah River from Augusta National, and its Tom Fazio design is considered one of the finest private courses in the Southeast
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: Par 5, 555 yards, a risk-reward hole with a creek crossing that tempts long hitters to go for the green in two, set in a natural amphitheater of pines and hardwoods
reveal.description: Sage Valley Golf Club is a Tom Fazio design located near the Georgia-South Carolina border, just minutes from Augusta National. The course rolls through dense pine forests and across ravines, providing a championship test that serves as the backdrop for the prestigious Junior Invitational each spring.

clues.tournamentHint: Has not hosted a major professional tournament; revered as one of Raynor's Southern masterpieces
clues.didYouKnow: Tom Doak led a meticulous restoration of the course in the 2000s, uncovering original Seth Raynor bunker shapes that had been buried and softened over eight decades of maintenance and renovation.

famousMoments: Tom Doak's restoration in the early 2000s is considered one of the finest course restorations in America, returning the layout to Raynor's original geometric bunker style
The club was originally established as a winter retreat for wealthy Northerners seeking refuge in the mild Charleston climate
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 5: Par 3, 165 yards, a classic Raynor Redan hole with a green angled sharply from front-right to back-left, defended by the signature deep bunker on the right side
reveal.description: Yeamans Hall Club is a Seth Raynor gem near Charleston, South Carolina, originally opened in 1925 as the centerpiece of an exclusive winter colony. Following a celebrated Tom Doak restoration, the course showcases Raynor's beloved template holes amid live oaks, Spanish moss, and the serene Lowcountry landscape.

clues.tournamentHint: Has not hosted a major professional tournament; regarded as one of the finest private clubs in Florida
clues.didYouKnow: Designer Jerry Pate won the 1976 U.S. Open as a rookie and famously leapt into the lake after winning the 1982 Players Championship — he channeled that competitive fire into creating one of Florida's most challenging and acclaimed courses.

famousMoments: Jerry Pate's design earned immediate national acclaim, vaulting into Golf Digest's top 100 list within years of opening
The course was built through a preserved Florida pine forest, with minimal earth-moving to maintain the natural character of the landscape
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 15: Par 3, 205 yards, a demanding long iron or hybrid over a pristine lake to a narrow green framed by towering slash pines and white sand bunkers
reveal.description: Calusa Pines Golf Club is a Jerry Pate design carved through a preserved native pine flatwoods forest in Naples, Florida. Unlike most Southwest Florida courses, Calusa Pines eschews waterfront real estate for dense tree-lined corridors and natural wetlands, creating a surprisingly dramatic and demanding test in the Sunshine State.

clues.tournamentHint: Has not hosted a major professional tournament; the only course the course named after himself
clues.didYouKnow: This course was so proud of this mountain course that he put his own name on it — the only course in his legendary portfolio to bear his name — calling it one of his finest creations.

famousMoments: Pete Dye personally oversaw construction of every hole, sculpting the Appalachian terrain into dramatic elevation changes and his trademark railroad tie features
The course rocketed into national top-100 rankings within years of opening, a rare feat for a course in West Virginia
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 18: Par 4, 475 yards, a stunning downhill finishing hole with panoramic mountain views, requiring a precise drive to set up an approach to a green perched on the edge of a steep hillside
reveal.description: Pete Dye Golf Club is the legendary architect's self-named masterpiece, carved through the Appalachian Mountains of West Virginia. The course features massive elevation changes, dramatic rock outcroppings, and Dye's signature creative bunkering, all set against sweeping views of the surrounding mountain ridges.

clues.tournamentHint: Has not hosted a major professional tournament; one of the oldest and most exclusive clubs on Long Island
clues.didYouKnow: The club's name comes from the course, a town in Kent, England, reflecting the strong British influence on early American golf. The course sits directly on the Atlantic Ocean and Hook Pond, giving it a true links character rare in the United States.

famousMoments: Willie Park Jr., a two-time Open Championship winner, designed the course using the natural dunes and ocean winds to create one of America's most authentic links experiences
The club has fiercely resisted modern development pressure, preserving its original routing and character for over 130 years
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 9: Par 3, 165 yards, a gorgeous short iron directly toward the Atlantic Ocean, with the beach and crashing waves serving as the backdrop behind a windswept green
reveal.description: Maidstone Club is one of America's oldest and most exclusive seaside courses, designed by Open Championship winner Willie Park Jr. in 1894. Set along the dunes and ponds of East Hampton, the course offers an authentic links experience with ocean holes, deep pot bunkers, and ever-present Atlantic winds.

clues.tournamentHint: Part of the course Dunes Golf Resort; has hosted the course Dunes Cup and various amateur events
clues.didYouKnow: This course is the only course at the course Dunes that routes primarily through coastal forest and meadows rather than along the oceanside cliffs, yet it consistently ranks among the resort's most beloved layouts.

famousMoments: Coore and Crenshaw routed the course through three distinct ecosystems — dense shore pine forest, open meadow, and coastal sand dunes — creating a journey unlike any other at Bandon
The course was the third to open at Bandon Dunes Resort, solidifying the property's reputation as America's greatest golf destination
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 14: Par 4, 348 yards, a short but deceptive hole that emerges from the forest into open dunes, offering a wide fairway but a fiercely contoured green that punishes imprecise approaches
reveal.description: Bandon Trails is a Coore and Crenshaw masterpiece that journeys through shore pine forest, meadows, and coastal dunes along the southern Oregon coast. The course offers a completely different experience from its oceanside siblings at Bandon Dunes Resort, trading crashing waves for peaceful forest corridors and dramatic reveals.

clues.tournamentHint: Part of the Bandon Dunes Golf Resort; hosts various amateur and resort events
clues.didYouKnow: Named for Charles Blair the course, the 'father of American golf architecture,' the course features reimagined versions of his most famous template holes including the Redan, Alps, Cape, and Biarritz — all built on a grand scale with enormous greens and wide fairways.

famousMoments: Tom Doak designed the course as an homage to C.B. Macdonald's design philosophy, recreating template holes on a massive scale with some greens exceeding 15,000 square feet
Old Macdonald's opening in 2010 completed the four-course lineup that made Bandon Dunes the undisputed top golf resort in America
reveal.signatureHole: Hole 4: Par 3, 175 yards, a faithful recreation of the famous Redan hole with a green angled diagonally away from the tee, featuring a steep front-to-back slope that funnels well-played shots toward the pin

clues.didYouKnow: One of the founding courses of the Myrtle Beach golf scene. Hosted the PGA Tour's Myrtle Beach Classic debut in 2024.

famousMoments: Thorbjorn Olesen broke the course record with 61 during the 2024 tournament
reveal.description: A classic Myrtle Beach resort course. Host of the PGA Tour Myrtle Beach Classic.

clues.tournamentHint: Rocket Mortgage Classic
clues.didYouKnow: A bent tree between the 7th and 8th hole on the North Course was bent by Native Americans as a marker for the original Indian Trail between Detroit and Pontiac.

reveal.description: A private golf club in Detroit. Donald Ross built the current 36-hole course. Hosts the Rocket Mortgage Classic on the North Course.

clues.tournamentHint: ISCO Championship
clues.didYouKnow: In 2024, the tournament set the record for the lowest 36-hole cut at a PGA Tour event at eight under par.

reveal.description: Hurstbourne Country Club in Louisville, Kentucky. The ISCO Championship moved to the club in 2025.

clues.tournamentHint: Barracuda Championship
clues.didYouKnow: The Barracuda Championship uses Modified Stableford scoring, the only format of its kind on the PGA Tour. The tournament moved to Old Greenwood in 2020.

famousMoments: 2024: Nick Dunlap earned his first win as a professional
Collin Morikawa birdied the last three holes to win in 2019
reveal.description: Tahoe Mountain Club's Old Greenwood course in Truckee, California. The Barracuda Championship moved here in 2020.
